text.title: agile-hybrid-approaches Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for agile-hybrid-approaches as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running daily standups alongside Gantt chart updates for the same work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Doubles reporting burden without improving either predictability or agility; the team serves the tools instead of the tools serving the team.

failure-mode.description: Calling a process "Hybrid" because it uses some agile terminology while keeping all waterfall gates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Agile in name only" loses the feedback-loop benefit of agile without gaining the predictability of predictive; worst of both worlds.

failure-mode.description: Dogmatic adherence to one methodology ("We MUST do pure Scrum").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No single approach fits all project types; forcing a misfit approach causes ceremony theater without the benefits of either model.

failure-mode.description: Maintaining both burn-down charts AND traditional Gantt for the same work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Doubles reporting burden without improving visibility; teams serve the tools instead of the tools serving the team.

failure-mode.description: Discovery → Implementation handoff without an explicit translation boundary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bakes in a translation tax (agile artifacts → waterfall inputs) that grows larger over time and is difficult to remove once established.
